solutions.solution: (C) Rocks and Rings diagram shown with Balls and Stones. Conclusions: I. Some stones are rocks. (✗) II. Some rings are rocks. (✗) III. Some balls are rocks. (✓) IV. No stone is rock. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E)
(d) Books, Tables, and Pencils diagram with Papers. Conclusions: I. Some books are pencils. (✗) II. Some pencils are tables. (✗) III. Some tables are books. (✗) IV. Some papers are tables. (✓)
(d) Desks, Combs, Mirrors, Pins diagram. Conclusions: I. Some pins are desks. (✗) II. Some combs are desks. (✗) III. Some pins are mirrors. (✗) IV. Some pins are either desks or mirrors. (✗)
(e) Buckets, Rods, Blades, Hammers diagram. Conclusions: I. Some buckets are hammers. (✓) II. Some rods are blades. (✓) III. All hammers are buckets. (✓) IV. All blades are rods. (✓)

(d) Trees, Bangles, Chairs, Flowers diagram. Conclusions: I. No tree is bangle. (✗) II. No chair is bangle. (✗) III. Some flowers are trees. (✗) IV. Some bangles are trees. (✗) Complementary pair (E-I)
(e) Girls, Cycles, Boys, Men diagram. Conclusions: I. Some boys are men. (✓) II. Some boys are cycles. (✗) III. Some boys are not cycles. (✓) IV. No cycle is girl. (✓)

(e) Method I: Scooters, Books, Girls, Motors diagram. Conclusions: I. Some girls are motors. (✗) II. Some girls are books. (✗) III. Some scooters are girls. (✓) IV. No girl is book. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E) Method II analysis with IEA rule application shows various statement validities and concludes either II or IV and III follow.

(b) Concentric circles diagram showing Dogs inside Rats inside Crows inside Parrots. Conclusions: I. All dogs are parrots. (✓) II. Some parrots are dogs. (✓) III. Some crows are dogs. (✓) IV. All rats are dogs. (✗)
